perturbation.original_text: 3. FEES 3.1 The Company will pay the Consultant basic remuneration for its services in the sum of $10,000 per month (the "Consulting Fee") commencing on the 1st day of February, 2020, and payable on the last day of each month thereafter up to and including the 31st day of January, 2025, together with any such increments thereto as the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors of the Company may from time to time determine. In addition, the Company will pay to the Consultant all reasonable expenses of the Consultant as agreed to from time to time which are incurred by the Consultant in delivery of the Services, based on monthly invoices submitted to the Company, including copies of all paid receipts; plus harmonized sales taxes or goods and services taxes, as the case may be, in addition to the Consulting Fees, which taxes will be remitted by the Consultant to the Canada Revenue Agency.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. FEES 3.1 The Company will pay the Consultant basic remuneration for its services in the sum of $10,000 per month (the "Consulting Fee") commencing on the 1st day of February, 2020, and payable on the last day of each month thereafter up to and including the 31st day of January, 2025, together with any such increments thereto as the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors of the Company may from time to time determine. In addition, the Company will pay to the Consultant all reasonable expenses of the Consultant as agreed to from time to time which are incurred by the Consultant in delivery of the Services, based on monthly invoices submitted to the Company, including copies of all paid receipts; plus harmonized sales taxes or goods and services taxes, as the case may be, in addition to the Consulting Fees, which taxes will be remitted by the Consultant to the Canada Revenue Agency. The invoices should be submitted every month, no later than 15 days after the completion of the services. Invoices submitted after this date are subject to a 10% reduction.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a penalty for late invoice submission (10% reduction). This contradicts the existing terms which state the "Consulting Fee" will be paid up to January 31, 2025, creating an inconsistency in the agreed compensation. This introduces legal uncertainty and potential disputes over payment amounts. The penalty for late submissions may affect the final fee amount, contradicting the specified "Consulting Fee".
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. FEES 3.1 The Company will pay the Consultant basic remuneration for its services in the sum of $10,000 per month (the "Consulting Fee") commencing on the 1st day of February, 2020, and payable on the last day of each month thereafter up to and including the 31st day of January, 2025, together with any such increments thereto as the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors of the Company may from time to time determine. In addition, the Company will pay to the Consultant all reasonable expenses of the Consultant as agreed to from time to time which are incurred by the Consultant in delivery of the Services, based on monthly invoices submitted to the Company, including copies of all paid receipts; plus harmonized sales taxes or goods and services taxes, as the case may be, in addition to the Consulting Fees, which taxes will be remitted by the Consultant to the Canada Revenue Agency. The invoices should be submitted every month, no later than 15 days after the completion of the services. Invoices submitted after this date are subject to a 10% reduction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a penalty (10% reduction) for late invoice submissions, which directly contradicts the original agreement to pay the "Consulting Fee" of $10,000 per month up to January 31, 2025. This inconsistency in payment terms creates a clear contradiction, making the final amount uncertain and potentially affecting the originally stated fixed fee. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.'

perturbation.original_text: 4. TERM AND RENEWAL 4.1 During the term of this Agreement, the Consultant shall provide its Services to the Company through its Principal, and the Consultant shall ensure that the Principal will be available to provide such Services to the Company in a timely manner. 4.2 The term of this Agreement is for a period of five (5) years (the "Term") commencing on the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the termination provisions of this Agreement, ending on January 31, 2025.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. TERM AND RENEWAL 4.1 During the term of this Agreement, the Consultant shall provide its Services to the Company through its Principal, and the Consultant shall ensure that the Principal will be available to provide such Services to the Company in a timely manner. 4.2 The term of this Agreement is for a period of five (5) years (the "Term") commencing on the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the termination provisions of this Agreement, the agreement will be automatically renewed unless a written notice of non-renewal is provided 60 days prior to the end date, ending on January 31, 2025.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding an automatic renewal clause to the term of the agreement. While 4.2 states it ends on January 31, 2025, this change makes it automatically renewable unless a notice is given. This creates uncertainty about the agreement's term and contradicts the clear end date, leading to potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. TERM AND RENEWAL 4.1 During the term of this Agreement, the Consultant shall provide its Services to the Company through its Principal, and the Consultant shall ensure that the Principal will be available to provide such Services to the Company in a timely manner. 4.2 The term of this Agreement is for a period of five (5) years (the "Term") commencing on the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the termination provisions of this Agreement, the agreement will be automatically renewed unless a written notice of non-renewal is provided 60 days prior to the end date, ending on January 31, 2025.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an automatic renewal clause in section 4.2 while simultaneously stating that the agreement ends on January 31, 2025. This directly contradicts the stated end date, creating uncertainty about the actual term of the agreement, thus satisfying the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5. TERMINATION 5.1 This Agreement can be terminated at any time prior to the expiry of the Term, as follows: (a) by the Consultant electing to give the Company not less than 3 months prior notice of such termination; (b) by the Company electing to give the Consultant 3 months prior notice of such termination along with a termination payment equal to the annual Consulting Fee; (c) by the Consultant electing to give the Company notice, in the event that there occurs a Change of Control (as defined below) within six (6) months of the effective date of such Change of Control, and if the Consultant so elects to terminate this Agreement, then the Consultant will be immediately entitled to a termination payment equal to CDN$750,000; and -2- (d) For the purpose of this clause, a Change of Control shall be deemed to have occurred when: (i) any person, entity or group becomes the beneficial owner of 20% or more of the combined voting power of the Company's then outstanding voting securities entitled to vote generally in the election of directors, and such person, entity or group uses such effective voting control to change a majority of the Board of Directors of the Company, either all at once or through any series of elections and appointments when considered together; or (ii) completion of the sale or other disposition by the Company of all or substantially all of the Company's assets or a reorganization or merger or consolidation of the Company with any other entity or corporation, other than: (A) a reorganization or merger or consolidation that would result in the voting securities of the Company outstanding immediately prior thereto continuing to represent, either by remaining outstanding or by being converted into voting securities of another entity, more than 50.1 % of the combined voting power of the voting securities of the Company or such other entity outstanding immediately after such reorganization or merger or consolidation; or (B) a reorganization or merger or consolidation effected to implement a recapitalization or reincorporation of the Company (or similar transaction) that does not result in a material change in beneficial ownership of the voting securities of the Company or its successor. 5.2 On any termination of this Agreement under Section 5.l (a), (b), or (c) all outstanding stock options granted to the Consultant shall be exercisable in accordance with the terms of the option agreements covering such grants. If there is any inconsistency between the terms of this Agreement and the terms of any stock option agreement governing the grant of any stock options to the Consultant or the Principal, then the terms of such stock option agreement shall prevail.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. TERMINATION 5.1 This Agreement can be terminated at any time prior to the expiry of the Term, as follows: (a) by the Consultant electing to give the Company not less than 3 months prior notice of such termination; (b) by the Company electing to give the Consultant 3 months prior notice of such termination along with a termination payment equal to the annual Consulting Fee, *unless the termination is due to poor performance by the consultant, in which case no payment is needed*; (c) by the Consultant electing to give the Company notice, in the event that there occurs a Change of Control (as defined below) within six (6) months of the effective date of such Change of Control, and if the Consultant so elects to terminate this Agreement, then the Consultant will be immediately entitled to a termination payment equal to CDN$750,000; and -2- (d) For the purpose of this clause, a Change of Control shall be deemed to have occurred when: (i) any person, entity or group becomes the beneficial owner of 20% or more of the combined voting power of the Company's then outstanding voting securities entitled to vote generally in the election of directors, and such person, entity or group uses such effective voting control to change a majority of the Board of Directors of the Company, either all at once or through any series of elections and appointments when considered together; or (ii) completion of the sale or other disposition by the Company of all or substantially all of the Company's assets or a reorganization or merger or consolidation of the Company with any other entity or corporation, other than: (A) a reorganization or merger or consolidation that would result in the voting securities of the Company outstanding immediately prior thereto continuing to represent, either by remaining outstanding or by being converted into voting securities of another entity, more than 50.1 % of the combined voting power of the voting securities of the Company or such other entity outstanding immediately after such reorganization or merger or consolidation; or (B) a reorganization or merger or consolidation effected to implement a recapitalization or reincorporation of the Company (or similar transaction) that does not result in a material change in beneficial ownership of the voting securities of the Company or its successor. 5.2 On any termination of this Agreement under Section 5.l (a), (b), or (c) all outstanding stock options granted to the Consultant shall be exercisable in accordance with the terms of the option agreements covering such grants. If there is any inconsistency between the terms of this Agreement and the terms of any stock option agreement governing the grant of any stock options to the Consultant or the Principal, then the terms of such stock option agreement shall prevail.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding termination payments. Originally, the company must pay a termination fee if terminating the consultant with 3 months notice. The added clause states that *unless the termination is due to poor performance by the consultant, in which case no payment is needed*. There's no definition of 'poor performance', thus creating ambiguity. This contradicts the original obligation and opens the door for disputes, as the consultant may claim it isn't due to poor performance, and the company may say it is. This is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. TERMINATION 5.1 This Agreement can be terminated at any time prior to the expiry of the Term, as follows: (a) by the Consultant electing to give the Company not less than 3 months prior notice of such termination; (b) by the Company electing to give the Consultant 3 months prior notice of such termination along with a termination payment equal to the annual Consulting Fee, *unless the termination is due to poor performance by the consultant, in which case no payment is needed*; (c) by the Consultant electing to give the Company notice, in the event that there occurs a Change of Control (as defined below) within six (6) months of the effective date of such Change of Control, and if the Consultant so elects to terminate this Agreement, then the Consultant will be immediately entitled to a termination payment equal to CDN$750,000; and -2- (d) For the purpose of this clause, a Change of Control shall be deemed to have occurred when: (i) any person, entity or group becomes the beneficial owner of 20% or more of the combined voting power of the Company's then outstanding voting securities entitled to vote generally in the election of directors, and such person, entity or group uses such effective voting control to change a majority of the Board of Directors of the Company, either all at once or through any series of elections and appointments when considered together; or (ii) completion of the sale or other disposition by the Company of all or substantially all of the Company's assets or a reorganization or merger or consolidation of the Company with any other entity or corporation, other than: (A) a reorganization or merger or consolidation that would result in the voting securities of the Company outstanding immediately prior thereto continuing to represent, either by remaining outstanding or by being converted into voting securities of another entity, more than 50.1 % of the combined voting power of the voting securities of the Company or such other entity outstanding immediately after such reorganization or merger or consolidation; or (B) a reorganization or merger or consolidation effected to implement a recapitalization or reincorporation of the Company (or similar transaction) that does not result in a material change in beneficial ownership of the voting securities of the Company or its successor. 5.2 On any termination of this Agreement under Section 5.l (a), (b), or (c) all outstanding stock options granted to the Consultant shall be exercisable in accordance with the terms of the option agreements covering such grants. If there is any inconsistency between the terms of this Agreement and the terms of any stock option agreement governing the grant of any stock options to the Consultant or the Principal, then the terms of such stock option agreement shall prevail.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the termination payment obligation for the company ('unless the termination is due to poor performance by the consultant, in which case no payment is needed'). This creates a direct conflict with the original clause (b) which mandates a termination payment equal to the annual Consulting Fee when the Company elects to terminate the consultant with 3 months' notice. The lack of definition for 'poor performance' creates ambiguity and the potential for disputes, making the original payment obligation uncertain and unenforceable in certain circumstances. This constitutes an in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies' definition provided.
